texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Pilot test of vacuum membrane distillation for seawater desalination on a ship
texts.text: Pilot test of vacuum membrane distillation for seawater desalination on a ship

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Ying Xu, Bao-ku Zhu, You-yi Xu*

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Abstract
texts.text: Abstract

texts.label: text
texts.orig: In this paper, a pilot vacuum membrane distillation device, using polypropylene hollow fiber, was designed and installed on a seagoing vessel to investigate the operating conditions. During operation, the incoming seawater was heated by the waste-heat generated from the vessel engine. It was found that this device could reach a desalting degree of 99.99% and a membrane flux of 5.4 kg/m 2 h at 55°C and -0.093 Mpa. The results obtained from this pilot test are in accord with the previous lab experiment results.
texts.text: In this paper, a pilot vacuum membrane distillation device, using polypropylene hollow fiber, was designed and installed on a seagoing vessel to investigate the operating conditions. During operation, the incoming seawater was heated by the waste-heat generated from the vessel engine. It was found that this device could reach a desalting degree of 99.99% and a membrane flux of 5.4 kg/m 2 h at 55°C and -0.093 Mpa. The results obtained from this pilot test are in accord with the previous lab experiment results.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Keywords: Vacuum membrane distillation; Polypropylene hollow fiber; Pilot test; Seawater; Desalination
texts.text: Keywords: Vacuum membrane distillation; Polypropylene hollow fiber; Pilot test; Seawater; Desalination

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 1. Introduction
texts.text: 1. Introduction

texts.label: text
texts.orig: During the last twenty years, an increasing number of studies have been made on hydrophilic microporous membranes in seawater desalination. Among these studies, the primary one was membrane distillation (MD). It is believed to have a great potential due to low energy requirements and low operational pressure as well as high construction  compactness  in  several  cases.  It  can achieve a desalting efficiency of 100%, while the reverse osmosis process (RO) can only reach a desalting efficiency of 95-98%. Compared with other membrane processes, an outstanding advan-
texts.text: During the last twenty years, an increasing number of studies have been made on hydrophilic microporous membranes in seawater desalination. Among these studies, the primary one was membrane distillation (MD). It is believed to have a great potential due to low energy requirements and low operational pressure as well as high construction  compactness  in  several  cases.  It  can achieve a desalting efficiency of 100%, while the reverse osmosis process (RO) can only reach a desalting efficiency of 95-98%. Compared with other membrane processes, an outstanding advan-

texts.label: footnote
texts.orig: *Corresponding author.
texts.text: *Corresponding author.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: tage of MD is that it can concentrate the water solution of a non-volatile solute to an extremely high concentration, even to the supersaturated state. However, RO can hardly be used to concentrate  high  concentration  solutions,  because  RO relates  to  the  solution's  osmotic  pressure;  the higher the concentration is, the higher the osmotic pressure. MD is not related to osmotic pressure; it can concentrate a solution as long as there is enough of  a  temperature  difference  and  the membrane has sufficient integrity. Moreover, MD has a higher flux than RO when the concentration is high [1].
texts.text: tage of MD is that it can concentrate the water solution of a non-volatile solute to an extremely high concentration, even to the supersaturated state. However, RO can hardly be used to concentrate  high  concentration  solutions,  because  RO relates  to  the  solution's  osmotic  pressure;  the higher the concentration is, the higher the osmotic pressure. MD is not related to osmotic pressure; it can concentrate a solution as long as there is enough of  a  temperature  difference  and  the membrane has sufficient integrity. Moreover, MD has a higher flux than RO when the concentration is high [1].

texts.text: Y. Xu et al. / Desalination 189 (2006) 165-169

texts.label: text
texts.orig: treatment  of  wastewater,  the  concentration  of liquid  food,  the  removal  of  alcohols  and  other VOCs from aqueous solutions, and even in wine productions [2]. But during the process of seawater desalting in MD, hot seawater on one side of the membrane (40-60°C) can easily exchange heat with the seawater on the other side (<30°C), which blocks the progress of MD, and slows down the speed of MD. This factor limits its application in  seawater desalting. To increase the speed of MD in desalination, a new kind of MD has been developed in recent years-vacuum membrane distillation (VMD). In VMD, seawater goes through on one side of the membrane and vacuum pumping is carried through on the other side of the membrane to speed up the evaporation of the seawater and the migration and diffusion of water vapor. Freshwater can be obtained by water vapor condensation. By increasing the temperature of inlet seawater (to 40-60°C), the flux of MD can be greatly raised [3]. In this work, a VMD device of polypropylene (PP) hollow fiber was designed and setup on a ship for studying operation conditions and seeking possible application in desalination of seawater, especially by utilizing the waste heat generated by a vessel's engine as the heat source for a VMD process.
texts.text: treatment  of  wastewater,  the  concentration  of liquid  food,  the  removal  of  alcohols  and  other VOCs from aqueous solutions, and even in wine productions [2]. But during the process of seawater desalting in MD, hot seawater on one side of the membrane (40-60°C) can easily exchange heat with the seawater on the other side (<30°C), which blocks the progress of MD, and slows down the speed of MD. This factor limits its application in  seawater desalting. To increase the speed of MD in desalination, a new kind of MD has been developed in recent years-vacuum membrane distillation (VMD). In VMD, seawater goes through on one side of the membrane and vacuum pumping is carried through on the other side of the membrane to speed up the evaporation of the seawater and the migration and diffusion of water vapor. Freshwater can be obtained by water vapor condensation. By increasing the temperature of inlet seawater (to 40-60°C), the flux of MD can be greatly raised [3]. In this work, a VMD device of polypropylene (PP) hollow fiber was designed and setup on a ship for studying operation conditions and seeking possible application in desalination of seawater, especially by utilizing the waste heat generated by a vessel's engine as the heat source for a VMD process.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 2. Experimental
texts.text: 2. Experimental

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 2.1. Materials and module
texts.text: 2.1. Materials and module

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The microporous PP hollow fiber membrane prepared by the authors [4,5] by a melt-stretching process has an inner diameter of 327 µm, wall thickness of 53 µm and its permeability of N 2 is 7.48×10 -2 cm 3 /cm 2 s cm Hg. Three bundles of PP hollow fiber membranes were sealed into a polyvinyl chloride (PVC) cylinder to form the module of VMD. Each PP hollow fiber bundle has 5,000 hollow fiber membranes and an effective length of 800 mm. The total membrane area was 12.3 m 2 . Before VMD operation, a water pressure of 0.3 Mpa was applied to the module in order to detect  the  leakage.  In  order  to  investigate  the influence  of  module  structure  on  the  VMD performance, two kinds of modules were adopted. For the first one, the modules were polypropylene hollow fiber  bundles  without  a  shell.  For  the second one, each polypropylene hollow fiber bundle was fixed into a shell of ABS.
texts.text: The microporous PP hollow fiber membrane prepared by the authors [4,5] by a melt-stretching process has an inner diameter of 327 µm, wall thickness of 53 µm and its permeability of N 2 is 7.48×10 -2 cm 3 /cm 2 s cm Hg. Three bundles of PP hollow fiber membranes were sealed into a polyvinyl chloride (PVC) cylinder to form the module of VMD. Each PP hollow fiber bundle has 5,000 hollow fiber membranes and an effective length of 800 mm. The total membrane area was 12.3 m 2 . Before VMD operation, a water pressure of 0.3 Mpa was applied to the module in order to detect  the  leakage.  In  order  to  investigate  the influence  of  module  structure  on  the  VMD performance, two kinds of modules were adopted. For the first one, the modules were polypropylene hollow fiber  bundles  without  a  shell.  For  the second one, each polypropylene hollow fiber bundle was fixed into a shell of ABS.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 2.2. Equipment and process
texts.text: 2.2. Equipment and process

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The structure and flow process of VMD and the actual VMD device are presented in Fig. 1. The seagoing vessel has two diesel engines, each having a power of 136 kW. The power of the engine is relatively small; the temperature of the level-one cooling water is about 65°C, while the temperature of the level-two cooling water is about 35°C. During operation, the level-one cooling water with flow of 1,800 kg/h was used as hot seawater for  the VMD process. For ease and convenience in installing and transporting, the device was designed to be assembled from separable parts.
texts.text: The structure and flow process of VMD and the actual VMD device are presented in Fig. 1. The seagoing vessel has two diesel engines, each having a power of 136 kW. The power of the engine is relatively small; the temperature of the level-one cooling water is about 65°C, while the temperature of the level-two cooling water is about 35°C. During operation, the level-one cooling water with flow of 1,800 kg/h was used as hot seawater for  the VMD process. For ease and convenience in installing and transporting, the device was designed to be assembled from separable parts.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Hot seawater circulation :  The  waste  heat calorifier heats the seawater. The hot seawater is pumped to the candle filter. After being filtered, it is delivered to the inner side of the PP hollow fiber membrane module and evaporates. The residual seawater back flows to the waste heat calorifier. This hot seawater circulation design greatly relieves the burden of the core-type filter. In addition,  the  design  sufficiently  utilizes  the  heat capacity of the hot seawater, which facilitates the higher temperature of VMD and speeds up the desalination.
texts.text: Hot seawater circulation :  The  waste  heat calorifier heats the seawater. The hot seawater is pumped to the candle filter. After being filtered, it is delivered to the inner side of the PP hollow fiber membrane module and evaporates. The residual seawater back flows to the waste heat calorifier. This hot seawater circulation design greatly relieves the burden of the core-type filter. In addition,  the  design  sufficiently  utilizes  the  heat capacity of the hot seawater, which facilitates the higher temperature of VMD and speeds up the desalination.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Condensation  system :  Cold  seawater  is pumped through an injector and the tube condenser by pump 1 and then flows out of the ship.
texts.text: Condensation  system :  Cold  seawater  is pumped through an injector and the tube condenser by pump 1 and then flows out of the ship.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Vacuum system : Once pump 1 is started and the cold seawater flows through the injector, the jet  current  generates  negative  pressure  in  the pump-line next to the injector. A high degree of vacuum is induced at the outside of the PP membrane module, the condenser as well as the freshwater tank. The vacuum can be removed by the release valve on the freshwater tank.
texts.text: Vacuum system : Once pump 1 is started and the cold seawater flows through the injector, the jet  current  generates  negative  pressure  in  the pump-line next to the injector. A high degree of vacuum is induced at the outside of the PP membrane module, the condenser as well as the freshwater tank. The vacuum can be removed by the release valve on the freshwater tank.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Freshwater collection system :  Water  vapor condenses in the condenser under the negative pressure and flows into the freshwater tank with the operation of a balance pipe. After the atmosphere valve opens, the freshwater can flow out.
texts.text: Freshwater collection system :  Water  vapor condenses in the condenser under the negative pressure and flows into the freshwater tank with the operation of a balance pipe. After the atmosphere valve opens, the freshwater can flow out.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Seawater refilling and releasing system : The seawater in the hot seawater box is continuously refilled through the leading valve. Concentrated seawater is released through the ball valve below the hot seawater box.
texts.text: Seawater refilling and releasing system : The seawater in the hot seawater box is continuously refilled through the leading valve. Concentrated seawater is released through the ball valve below the hot seawater box.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 3. Results and discussion
texts.text: 3. Results and discussion

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 3.1. Effect of temperature on the flux in the pilot test and comparison between the lab experiment and the pilot test
texts.text: 3.1. Effect of temperature on the flux in the pilot test and comparison between the lab experiment and the pilot test

texts.label: text
texts.orig: In  the  pilot  test,  the  feed  speed  was  set  to 500 l/h. As is shown in Table 1, the membrane distillation  flux  increased  with  increasing  the temperature of feeding seawater. The membrane distillation flux reached 5.4 kg/m 2 h at 55°C; correspondingly, the distillation flux of this device was 66.0 kg/h. In our previous lab experiment with VMD, an aqueous solution of about 35 g/l NaCl was prepared by dissolving edible salt and iodide in deionized water as simulated seawater. The feed speed was set to 75 l/h. The downstream vacuum degree was held to a value as high as possible under the operation temperature. Fig. 2 indicates that the pilot test results matched the lab experiment results; the fluxes in both lab experiment and pilot test increased with the temperature.
texts.text: In  the  pilot  test,  the  feed  speed  was  set  to 500 l/h. As is shown in Table 1, the membrane distillation  flux  increased  with  increasing  the temperature of feeding seawater. The membrane distillation flux reached 5.4 kg/m 2 h at 55°C; correspondingly, the distillation flux of this device was 66.0 kg/h. In our previous lab experiment with VMD, an aqueous solution of about 35 g/l NaCl was prepared by dissolving edible salt and iodide in deionized water as simulated seawater. The feed speed was set to 75 l/h. The downstream vacuum degree was held to a value as high as possible under the operation temperature. Fig. 2 indicates that the pilot test results matched the lab experiment results; the fluxes in both lab experiment and pilot test increased with the temperature.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 3.2. Desalting degree
texts.text: 3.2. Desalting degree

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Desalting degree is another important factor for  VMD. In  theory,  if  the  membrane  is  not broken, the desalting rate of VMD can be 100%. To prolong the life of the PP membrane and membrane module, the broken rate of membrane must be reduced. In this study, the PP hollow fiber membranes were specifically prepared for VMD desalination processes and the wall thickness (e.g. the membrane thickness) was as large as 53 µm to improve the membrane's strength and durability.
texts.text: Desalting degree is another important factor for  VMD. In  theory,  if  the  membrane  is  not broken, the desalting rate of VMD can be 100%. To prolong the life of the PP membrane and membrane module, the broken rate of membrane must be reduced. In this study, the PP hollow fiber membranes were specifically prepared for VMD desalination processes and the wall thickness (e.g. the membrane thickness) was as large as 53 µm to improve the membrane's strength and durability.

texts.text: Fig. 2. Results comparison between lab experiment and pilot test.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Throughout a five month experiment, the desalting degree of this device still held to 99.99%. The salt content in freshwater was below 3 mg/l and meets the requirements of drinking water. Despite the durability of the membrane, one outlet at the bottom of the membrane distillation chamber is also designed to ensure that low-salt-content freshwater can be obtained even when some fibers are broken. If the PP membrane breaks during the desalting  process,  the  seeping  seawater  in  the membrane distillation  chamber  can  flow  out through vacuum generated by a water injector, without interrupting the desalting process, thus the desalting rate will not be affected greatly.
texts.text: Throughout a five month experiment, the desalting degree of this device still held to 99.99%. The salt content in freshwater was below 3 mg/l and meets the requirements of drinking water. Despite the durability of the membrane, one outlet at the bottom of the membrane distillation chamber is also designed to ensure that low-salt-content freshwater can be obtained even when some fibers are broken. If the PP membrane breaks during the desalting  process,  the  seeping  seawater  in  the membrane distillation  chamber  can  flow  out through vacuum generated by a water injector, without interrupting the desalting process, thus the desalting rate will not be affected greatly.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 4. Conclusions
texts.text: 4. Conclusions

texts.label: text
texts.orig: After  a  five  month  experiment  of  seawater desalination on a seagoing vessel, the designed device still displayed a high degree of desalting and a high usage of waste heat. The membrane distillation flux reached 5.4 kg/m 2 h at 55°C and -0.093 Mpa while the desalting rate reached 99.99%. The results indicate that this device can be developed into practical applications in preparation of freshwater from seawater, especially in the field where sufficient waste heat is available.
texts.text: After  a  five  month  experiment  of  seawater desalination on a seagoing vessel, the designed device still displayed a high degree of desalting and a high usage of waste heat. The membrane distillation flux reached 5.4 kg/m 2 h at 55°C and -0.093 Mpa while the desalting rate reached 99.99%. The results indicate that this device can be developed into practical applications in preparation of freshwater from seawater, especially in the field where sufficient waste heat is available.
